seo.title: About Yordan Ivanov - Head of Data at Dext
seo.description: 15 years in data. Three unicorns. Four acquisitions. Head of Data at Dext. I coach data engineers on the non-technical side of the job.

content.content:
I've spent 15 years in data, most of it at high-growth companies. I led data teams through three unicorns and four acquisitions. These days I'm Head of Data at Dext, where I run the strategy behind our global data platform.

## Scaling Through Change

Three unicorns and four acquisitions teach you a lot about what breaks when companies grow fast. Ownership changes, KPIs shift, reporting needs to stay stable while everything around it moves. I've done this enough times to know what works and what doesn't.

## What I've Built

At Dext, I run the data strategy that powers our global capabilities. Some numbers that matter:

*   Cut infrastructure costs by 50% through better planning and pipeline execution.
*   Built systems that handled 150% growth while improving report speeds by 83%.
*   Unified commercial data models so every department works from the same source of truth.

![](/images/journey.png)

## How I Think About Leadership

I pick reliable, proven technology over shiny new tools. I care about measurable impact, not activity. Every technical decision should connect to something the business actually needs. And I believe in radical clarity: if your stakeholders can't understand your data strategy, it's not a strategy.

## Writing and Coaching

I write [_Data Gibberish_](https://www.datagibberish.com/), a newsletter for data engineers who want to get better at the non-technical side of the job. I also [coach](/coaching) senior data engineers and new data leads on stakeholder communication, promotion business cases, and career strategy.
